description_long: This house features a traditional European facade with two distinct sections, each with its own roofline and window arrangement. The left section has a steeply pitched gable roof with stepped edges and dormer windows, while the right section has a slightly lower, tiled roof with a large dormer and a flat parapet. The exterior walls are finished in a faded yellow plaster, showing signs of age and weathering, and the ground floor includes several shopfronts or entrances with awnings. Windows are arranged in both symmetrical and asymmetrical patterns, with some featuring decorative trim and small balconies. The side and rear walls are plain, with minimal detailing and a smooth, light-colored plaster finish. The overall appearance suggests a historic, urban residential building, likely from the late 19th or early 20th century, commonly found in Central or Eastern European cities.
description: A historic, plaster-finished urban house with a steep gable roof, dormer windows, and decorative window trim, featuring shopfronts at street level.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed facade with windows, balconies, and shopfronts beneath a tiled roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the stepped gable roof and plain, unadorned plaster wall.
description_view_2: Rear or top-down view displaying the tiled roof surfaces and the plain, light-colored back walls.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear view of the stepped gable and smooth plaster wall, lacking windows or decoration.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with some symmetrical rows and decorative trim
attribute_entryway_design: Ground-level shopfronts and residential doors with awnings, minimal steps, and some decorative trim
